messages.content:

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
  "opinion": "As a native-born American who's been around since before social media became what it is today, I can tell you that fake content really gets under my skin. You know, when I'm not fixing pipes or playing videogames on those rare occasions when I log onto Facebook, I read novels and watch cinema, but even then, seeing all this nonsense online is frustrating. It's like people are just throwing stuff out there without thinking.\n\nI think the best way to deal with it is to be more cautious about where you get your information from. Back in my day as a plumber, we learned to check measurements three times before trusting them once. Same thing here: don't trust everything at face value.\n\nAlso, I believe everyone should take responsibility for their own digital diet. If you see something that looks off, report it and maybe block the source if they keep posting this stuff. It's not just about the content itself but also how we react to it matters.",

My moderate openness allows me to keep an open mind about new things but not at the expense of common sense.\n\nThe fact that my reading habits include novels helps balance out my low agreeableness by being a source of perspective without getting into social debates where fake content might arise.",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a native-born American who's been around since before social media became what it is today, I can tell you that fake content really gets under my skin. You know, when I'm not fixing pipes or playing videogames on those rare occasions when I log onto Facebook, I read novels and watch cinema, but even then, seeing all this nonsense online is frustrating. It's like people are just throwing stuff out there without thinking.

I think the best way to deal with it is to be more cautious about where you get your information from. Back in my day as a plumber, we learned to check measurements three times before trusting them once. Same thing here: don't trust everything at face value.

Also, I believe everyone should take responsibility for their own digital diet. If you see something that looks off, report it and maybe block the source if they keep posting this stuff. It's not just about the content itself but also how we react to it matters.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
Based on my background as a tradesperson with low conscientiousness scores in terms of planning but high extraversion when socializing offline, I approach fake online content much like handling physical materials: be skeptical and verify everything. My moderate openness allows me to keep an open mind about new things but not at the expense of common sense.

The fact that my reading habits include novels helps balance out my low agreeableness by being a source of perspective without getting into social debates where fake content might arise.
response_parsed.safety_notes: None needed as opinion is general and non-controversial
